# UC-10.18.4 · Broadcom Symantec SEP Application and Device Control Policy Violations

## Description

Application and Device Control policies stop unapproved software and removable media from exfiltrating data or running untrusted binaries. Aggregating policy violations reveals users or systems routinely fighting controls and highlights policy gaps.

## Value

Strengthens data-loss prevention at the endpoint and gives auditors evidence that technical enforcement of software and USB restrictions is operating.

## Implementation

(1) Ensure SEPM logs Application and Device Control events. (2) Normalize `Policy_Name` and descriptions. (3) Group by business unit via CMDB. (4) Separate expected test machines with a lookup. (5) Weekly review with desktop engineering for top violating hosts.

## SPL

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:security:file" earliest=-7d
| eval host=coalesce(Computer_Name, computer_name, host)
| eval desc=coalesce(Event_Description, event_description, _raw)
| where match(lower(desc), "(?i)application control|device control|usb|removable|blocked by policy|peripheral")
| eval policy=coalesce(Policy_Name, policy_name, "unknown")
| stats count by host policy desc
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (violations by policy), table (host × policy × count), pie (block vs audit-only if action field exists).

## References

- [Documentation — Symantec Endpoint Protection Application Control](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/AddOns/released/SymantecEP/About)
- [Splunkbase — Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2772)
